name: Git commands
description: Everyday Git tasks mapped to the command that does them — staging, committing, branching, undoing, inspecting history, and working with remotes. Most cards give a task and ask for the command; a few run in reverse, showing a command and asking what it does.
primer:
Each task below maps to the command that performs it. Mentally `git ` precedes each entry.

## Staging & commit

- **Stage all changes** (new, modified, deleted) — `git add -A` (or `git add .`).
- **Commit staged changes with a message** — `git commit -m "message"`. One-step stage tracked + commit: `git commit -am "..."`.
- **Amend the last commit** (message or contents) — `git commit --amend`. Folds in newly staged changes; avoid on already-pushed commits.

## Branch & merge

- **Create a branch and switch to it** — `git switch -c <name>` (older: `git checkout -b <name>`).
- **Switch to an existing branch** — `git switch <name>` (older: `git checkout <name>`).
- **Merge another branch into the current one** — `git merge <branch>` (creates a merge commit unless fast-forward).
- **Replay commits on top of another branch (linear history)** — `git rebase <branch>`. Rewrites commit hashes.
- **Apply one specific commit onto the current branch** — `git cherry-pick <commit>`.
- **Delete a merged local branch** — `git branch -d <name>` (`-D` force-deletes unmerged).

## Undo & inspect

- **Undo last commit, keep changes staged** — `git reset --soft HEAD~1` (`--mixed`, the default, leaves them unstaged).
- **Undo last commit, discard changes** — `git reset --hard HEAD~1` (destructive; recoverable only via reflog).
- **Discard uncommitted changes to a file** — `git restore <file>` (older: `git checkout -- <file>`).
- **Unstage a file but keep its changes** — `git restore --staged <file>` (older: `git reset HEAD <file>`).
- **Reverse a commit with a new commit** — `git revert <commit>` (safe way to undo something already pushed).
- **Compact one-line history** — `git log --oneline` (add `--graph --all` for topology).
- **Show unstaged changes** — `git diff` (`git diff --staged` compares index vs last commit).
- **State of working tree and index** — `git status`.
- **Shelve uncommitted changes** — `git stash`; reapply most recent with `git stash pop` (`git stash apply` keeps it on the stack).
- **History of where HEAD has pointed** (recover lost commits after a reset) — `git reflog`.

## Remotes

- **Download new commits without merging** — `git fetch` (updates remote-tracking branches like `origin/main`).
- **Fetch and integrate into the current branch** — `git pull` (fetch + merge, or `--rebase`).
- **Send local commits to the remote** — `git push` (first push of a new branch: `git push -u origin <name>`).
- **Copy a remote repo to your machine** — `git clone <url>` (sets up `origin`, checks out the default branch).

cards.term: Stage all changes in the working tree
cards.definition: git add -A (or git add .). Stages new, modified and deleted files so they are included in the next commit. The -A form also catches deletions across the whole repo.

cards.term: Record the staged changes as a commit with a message
cards.definition: git commit -m "message". Creates a commit from whatever is staged. Use git commit -am "..." to stage tracked modifications and commit in one step.

cards.term: Undo the last commit but keep the changes staged
cards.definition: git reset --soft HEAD~1. Moves HEAD back one commit; the changes from that commit remain in the index, ready to recommit. Use --mixed (the default) to keep them unstaged instead.

cards.term: Undo the last commit and discard its changes entirely
cards.definition: git reset --hard HEAD~1. Moves HEAD back one commit and resets the working tree to match, throwing away the changes. Destructive — the work is lost unless reachable via the reflog.

cards.term: Amend the most recent commit (message or contents)
cards.definition: git commit --amend. Replaces the last commit with a new one, folding in any newly staged changes and letting you edit the message. Avoid on commits already pushed and shared.

cards.term: Discard uncommitted changes to a tracked file
cards.definition: git restore <file> (or the older git checkout -- <file>). Resets the file in the working tree to its last committed state. The change is lost.

cards.term: Unstage a file but keep its changes
cards.definition: git restore --staged <file> (or the older git reset HEAD <file>). Removes the file from the index while leaving the working-tree edits intact.

cards.term: Create a new branch and switch to it
cards.definition: git switch -c <name> (or the older git checkout -b <name>). Creates the branch from the current HEAD and checks it out in one command.

cards.term: Switch to an existing branch
cards.definition: git switch <name> (or the older git checkout <name>). Moves HEAD to the named branch and updates the working tree to match it.

cards.term: Merge another branch into the current one
cards.definition: git merge <branch>. Combines the named branch's history into the current branch, creating a merge commit unless a fast-forward is possible.

cards.term: Replay your commits on top of another branch (linear history)
cards.definition: git rebase <branch>. Re-applies the current branch's commits one by one onto the tip of <branch>, producing a linear history without a merge commit. Rewrites commit hashes.

cards.term: Delete a local branch that is already merged
cards.definition: git branch -d <name>. Deletes the branch; Git refuses if it holds unmerged commits. Use -D (capital) to force-delete regardless.

cards.term: Show the commit history compactly, one line each
cards.definition: git log --oneline. Prints each commit as an abbreviated hash and subject line. Add --graph --all to visualise branch topology.

cards.term: Show what has changed but is not yet staged
cards.definition: git diff. Shows the difference between the working tree and the index. Use git diff --staged to compare the index against the last commit instead.

cards.term: Show the current state of the working tree and index
cards.definition: git status. Lists staged, unstaged and untracked files, plus the current branch and its relation to the upstream.

cards.term: Temporarily shelve uncommitted changes
cards.definition: git stash. Saves the working-tree and index changes onto a stack and reverts to a clean state. Restore them later with git stash pop.

cards.term: Reapply the most recently stashed changes
cards.definition: git stash pop. Applies the top stash entry back onto the working tree and removes it from the stash stack. Use git stash apply to keep it on the stack.

cards.term: Create a new commit that reverses a given commit
cards.definition: git revert <commit>. Records a new commit undoing the named commit's changes, leaving history intact — the safe way to undo something already pushed.

cards.term: Download new commits from the remote without merging
cards.definition: git fetch. Updates your remote-tracking branches (e.g. origin/main) but does not change your local branches or working tree. Merge or rebase afterwards.

cards.term: Fetch from the remote and integrate into the current branch
cards.definition: git pull. Runs git fetch then merges (or, with --rebase, rebases) the upstream changes into your current branch.

cards.term: Send your local commits to the remote
cards.definition: git push. Uploads commits on the current branch to its upstream. The first push of a new branch needs git push -u origin <name> to set the upstream.

cards.term: Copy a remote repository to your machine
cards.definition: git clone <url>. Downloads the full repository, sets up origin as the remote, and checks out the default branch.

cards.term: git cherry-pick <commit>
cards.definition: Applies the changes from a single specific commit onto the current branch as a new commit. Useful for pulling one fix from another branch without merging everything.
cards.summary: Apply one commit's changes onto the current branch.

cards.term: git reflog
cards.definition: Shows the history of where HEAD has pointed — including commits orphaned by resets or rebases. The recovery tool for getting back work that seems lost after a hard reset.
cards.summary: Log of HEAD movements; recover lost commits.
